zh.lifeSummary: 當Noah Cartwright於大約1799年出生在美國北卡羅來納州吉爾福特時，他的父親John Cartwright30歲，母親Elizabeth Mears36歲。他於1819年2月4日與Elizabeth Overman在美國印第安納州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他於1850年住在美國印第安納州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾且於1860年住在美國印第安納州克拉克門羅 堂史普。他去世於1870年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Noah Cartwright于大约1799年出生在美国北卡罗来纳州吉爾福特时，他的父亲John Cartwright30岁，母亲Elizabeth Mears36岁。他于1819年2月4日与Elizabeth Overman在美国印第安纳州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他于1850年住在美国印第安纳州克拉克福森維爾 堂史普福森維爾且于1860年住在美国印第安纳州克拉克門羅 堂史普。他去世于1870年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Cartwright
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): occupational name from Middle English cart(e) ‘cart’ + wright(e) ‘craftsman’ (Old English cræt or Old Norse kartr + Old English wyrhta), for a cartwright, a maker of carts.
Variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Gathright
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Cartwright  .

variants.name: Cartright
meanings: Probably also a variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Courtright
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Kortrijk: habitational name from the city of Kortrijk in Flanders, in French known as Courtrai. Compare Cartwright , Cortright , Courtwright , Curtright , Cutright , and Kortright .
Perhaps also an altered form of English   Cartwright  .

History: The progenitors of the (majority of the) Courtrights (and Cortrights, Kortrights, etc.) were the brothers Jan and Michiel Bastiansen van Kortryk from Leerdam in the Netherlands, who arrived with their families to New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1663. The descendants of one or another brother also bear the surnames Kortright, Kortwright, Cortright, Co(u)rtwright, Cartwright, and Cu(r)tright.

nameMeanings.name: Noah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the biblical character whose family was the only one saved from the great Flood ordained by God to destroy mankind because of its wickedness. The origin of the name is far from certain; in the Bible it is implied that it means ‘rest’ (Genesis 5:29, ‘and he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the Lord hath cursed’). One tradition indeed explains it as derived from the Hebrew root meaning ‘to comfort’ (  see  Nahum  ) with the final consonant dropped. It was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, and is presently enjoying a revival throughout the English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Historical Information: 1801: Knox, Indiana Territory, United States 1801: Clark, Indiana Territory, United States 1816: Clark, Indiana, United States

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.
Historical Boundaries:  1827:  Clark, Indiana, United States

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

historicEvents.title: First State Fair
historicEvents.description: The first state fair in North Carolina was held in Raleigh and was put on by the North Carolina State Agricultural Society in 1853. The fair has been continuous except for during the American Civil War and Reconstruction and WWII.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Roanoke Island
historicEvents.description: On February 7, 1862, General Burnside's expedition started with the Battle of Roanoke Island. The battle was mostly fought by the Union and Confederate Navy's. This was a Union victory.
historicEvents.urlTitle: National Park Service- The Battle of Roanoke Island

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

en.lifeSummary: When Noah Cartwright was born about 1799, in Guilford, North Carolina, United States, his father, John Cartwright, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth Mears, was 36. He married Elizabeth Overman on 4 February 1819, in Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He lived in Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, United States in 1850 and Monroe Township, Clark, Indiana, United States in 1860. He died before 1870.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Overman le 4 février 1819, à Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il a vécu à Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, États-Unis en 1850 et à Monroe Township, Clark, Indiana, États-Unis en 1860. Il est décédé avant 1870.

ja.Guilford, North Carolina, United States: アメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州グイルフォード
ja.lifeSummary: Noah Cartwrightが約1799年にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州グイルフォードで生まれた時，父John Cartwrightは30歳，母Elizabeth Mearsは36歳であった。1819年2月4日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークジェフエルソンヴィル トウンシュイップジェフエルソンヴィルでElizabeth Overmanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1850年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークジェフエルソンヴィル トウンシュイップジェフエルソンヴィルに，1860年にはアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州クラークモンロー トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼は1870年以前に死去した。

ko.Jeffersonville, Clark, Indiana, United S: 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 제퍼손빌 타운시프 제퍼손빌
ko.lifeSummary: Noah Cartwright(이)가 대략 1799년 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주 길퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Cartwright(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Mears(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1819년 2월 4일 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 제퍼손빌 타운시프 제퍼손빌에서 Elizabeth Overman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 1850년도에 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 제퍼손빌 타운시프 제퍼손빌에서, 1860년도에 미국 인디애나 주 클라르크 먼로 타운시프에서 살았다. 그는 1870년 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Noah Cartwright nasceu aproximadamente 1799, em Guilford, Carolina do Norte, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, John Cartwright, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mears, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Overman em 4 de fevereiro de 1819, em Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele viveu em Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América em 1850 e em Monroe Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América em 1860. Ele faleceu antes 1870.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Noah Cartwright родился около 1799 г. в местности гуилфорд, Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени John Cartwright было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mears, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Overman 4 февраля 1819 г. в местности джеффирсонвилл, джеффирсонвилл тоуншип, кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он жил в местности джеффирсонвилл, джеффирсонвилл тоуншип, кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1850 году и в местности монро тоуншип, кларк, Индиана, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году. Он умер перед 1870 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Noah Cartwright nació aproximadamente 1799, en Guilford, Carolina del Norte, Estados Unidos, su papá, John Cartwright, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mears, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Overman el 4 de febrero de 1819, en Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Vivió en Jeffersonville, Jeffersonville Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos en 1850 y en Monroe Township, Clark, Indiana, Estados Unidos en 1860. Murió antes de 1870.
